description_long: This object is a classic rotary dial corded telephone, featuring a robust rectangular base with a slightly sloped top surface. The handset rests horizontally on top, supported by two cradles at each end, and is connected to the base by a coiled cord. The rotary dial is prominently positioned on the front right side of the base, with visible finger holes and number markings. The surface appears to be made of a dark, slightly worn plastic or Bakelite material, giving it a vintage, utilitarian look. The overall design is compact and functional, with minimal decorative elements, emphasizing durability and ease of use. The coiled cord and rotary dial are key interactive features, typical of mid-20th-century telephones.
description: A vintage rotary dial corded telephone with a dark, slightly worn finish, a coiled cord, and a robust rectangular base. The handset rests on top, and the rotary dial is clearly visible on the front.
description_view_0: Side view showing the handset resting on the base and the rotary dial partially visible.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the full handset, both cradles, and the coiled cord extending from the right.
description_view_2: Opposite side view with the coiled cord and handset clearly visible, rotary dial partially obscured.
description_view_3: Angled front view displaying the rotary dial, coiled cord, and both cradles supporting the handset.
